frameworks.name: neural distance-to-bound
frameworks.one_line: distance from a linear classifier boundary in neural activation space predicts reaction time

frameworks.name: signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: decision-boundary framework extended from behavior to multivariate neural activity

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis (neural decoding)
frameworks.one_line: linear classifiers on high-dimensional neural patterns instantiate decision boundaries

frameworks.name: Fechner's inner psychophysics
frameworks.one_line: historical program of mapping mental quantities onto brain states

core_question: Can linear-classifier distances in neural activation space serve as an 'inner psychophysics' linking brain activity to behavior?
key_assumptions: linear classifiers and decision-boundary observers share an architecture
neural activity lies in a high-dimensional activation space amenable to linear decoding
distance from a decision boundary monotonically maps to behavioral performance
key_findings: linear-classifier distance in activation space can predict reaction time under signal detection theory
the neural distance-to-bound approach is general and simple to implement
interpretations: MVPA-based linear decoding provides a starting point for an inner psychophysics linking mind, brain, and behavior
decision-boundary models give a principled bridge between neural decoding and behavioral measures
